SEARCH
Package com.example.cmput301_app.database

Class NotificationDB

java.lang.Object
com.example.cmput301_app.database.NotificationDB
public class NotificationDB extends Object
Handles all Firestore database operations for Notification objects. This class is the sole point of contact between the application and Firestore for notification-related data. It follows the repository pattern, meaning no other class should read or write notification data to Firestore directly — all operations go through this class. Notification documents are stored in the "notifications" collection using a Firestore auto-generated document ID as the notification ID. Outstanding issues: - Actual push notification delivery via Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) is not yet implemented. This class handles only the persistence of notification data to Firestore. - deleteNotification() and getNotificationsByOrganizer() are not yet implemented as they are not required for the halfway checkpoint.

  • Constructor Details

    • NotificationDB

      public NotificationDB()
      Constructs a NotificationDB and initializes the Firestore instance.

  • Method Details

    • createNotification

      public void createNotification(Notification notification, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnSuccessListener<Notification> successListener, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnFailureListener failureListener)
      Creates a new notification document in the "notifications" collection. Firestore auto-generates the document ID, which is then set back on the Notification object and passed to the success listener.
      Parameters:
      notification - the Notification object to store in Firestore
      successListener - called with the updated Notification object containing the generated ID
      failureListener - called if the operation fails

    • getNotification

      public void getNotification(String notificationId, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnSuccessListener<Notification> successListener, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnFailureListener failureListener)
      Fetches a notification document from Firestore by notification ID and returns it as a Notification object via the success listener.
      Parameters:
      notificationId - the ID of the notification to fetch
      successListener - called with the Notification object if found, or null if not found
      failureListener - called if the operation fails

    • getNotificationsByEvent

      public void getNotificationsByEvent(String eventId, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnSuccessListener<List<Notification>> successListener, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnFailureListener failureListener)
      Fetches all notification documents associated with a specific event and returns them as a list of Notification objects via the success listener. Used to display the notification history for an event.
      Parameters:
      eventId - the ID of the event whose notifications to fetch
      successListener - called with the list of Notification objects
      failureListener - called if the operation fails

    • getAllNotifications

      public void getAllNotifications(com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnSuccessListener<List<Notification>> successListener, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnFailureListener failureListener)
      Fetches all notification documents from Firestore and returns them as a list of Notification objects via the success listener. Used to populate the admin notification log screen (US 03.08.01).
      Parameters:
      successListener - called with the list of Notification objects
      failureListener - called if the operation fails

    • deleteNotification

      public void deleteNotification(String notificationId, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnSuccessListener<Void> successListener, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnFailureListener failureListener)
      TODO: Not required for the halfway checkpoint. Deletes a notification document from Firestore by notification ID. To be implemented in a future sprint.
      Parameters:
      notificationId - the ID of the notification to delete
      successListener - called when the operation completes successfully
      failureListener - called if the operation fails

    • getNotificationsByOrganizer

      public void getNotificationsByOrganizer(String organizerId, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnSuccessListener<List<Notification>> successListener, com.google.android.gms.tasks.OnFailureListener failureListener)
      TODO: Not required for the halfway checkpoint. Fetches all notification documents sent by a specific organizer and returns them as a list of Notification objects via the success listener. Used for admin notification log filtering (US 03.08.01). To be implemented in a future sprint.
      Parameters:
      organizerId - the device ID of the organizer whose notifications to fetch
      successListener - called with the list of Notification objects
      failureListener - called if the operation fails
